spec_match.rationale: The page now includes a native dialog top-layer probe that checks whether focus inside a modal dialog leaks :focus-within to an ancestor, but the visible popover/dialog examples still use custom div overlays rather than native top-layer elements.

interactive.rationale: The page exposes click, hover, focus, close, and log interactions, and now records the native focus-within boundary probe in the event log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The text promises Chrome 149 top-layer boundary behavior; the repaired focus probe now matches that claim, while the hover visual still needs a native popover/dialog implementation to prove the boundary.

edge_cases.rationale: The probe covers the :focus-within modal-dialog case, but does not yet behavior-test :hover or :active across a native top-layer element.

references.rationale: The feature index links to the ChromeStatus entry for feature 6296574159355904.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the showcase variables and high-contrast status/log styling.

openQuestions.title: Use native top-layer elements for hover and active cases
openQuestions.detail: The repaired focus probe uses a real modal dialog, but the visible popover and dialog demos are custom div overlays. Replace or supplement them with native popover/dialog elements so :hover, :active, and :focus-within are all tested against the actual top layer.

openQuestions.title: Re-review repaired live probe in chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: The native focus-within boundary probe was added on 2026-06-04. Re-run this route in chrome-devtools-mcp, open the dialog, focus the input, inspect the log, and verify console state before treating this critique as current.

summary: 2026-06-04 maintenance replaced an unrelated CSS.supports(':focus-within') placeholder with a native modal-dialog boundary probe. chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable in this session, so browser click-through re-review is still required.
